mission.title: Install and Configure Flowise
mission.description: Flowise is an AI platform that allows users to build AI agents visually. It is ideal for developers looking to create and manage AI workflows without deep programming knowledge. Key trade-offs include the need for Node.js and Docker for local development and the complexity of managing Kubernetes resources.

steps.title: Install the Flowise CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Flowise command-line interface globally on your machine, which is necessary for managing Flowise applications.

```bash

npm install -g flowise@3.0.13

```

> Install the specific version of Flowise CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create flowise-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials flowise-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This allows kubectl to interact with your cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Install Flowise on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys Flowise on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add flowise https://flowiseai.github.io/flowise-charts

helm install flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise --create-namespace

```

> Add the Flowise Helm repository.
> Install Flowise in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Flowise deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment flowise --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=5 --namespace flowise

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml --namespace flowise

```

> Set up autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Apply monitoring configurations.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description: Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources used. Monitor your usage to avoid unexpected charges.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Flowise
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Flowise deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

helm uninstall flowise --namespace flowise

gcloud container clusters delete flowise-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This will remove the Flowise application.
> This will delete the entire cluster and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Flowise
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade Flowise to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp flowise-backup:/data /local/backup

helm upgrade flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise

kubectl get pods --namespace flowise

```

> Backup your Flowise data.
> Upgrade Flowise to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running correctly after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<PORT>: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that your kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials flowise-cluster --region=us-central1' to refresh your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image "flowise:3.0.13": rpc error: code = NotFound'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified image version does not exist in the repository.

**Fix:**
Check the image version in your Helm chart and ensure it matches the available versions.

**Affected versions:** <= 1.30

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes returns no nodes'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the cluster was not created successfully or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Verify your cluster creation command and check your cloud provider's console for cluster status.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the Flowise service'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Flowise service is not running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get services --namespace flowise' to check the service status and logs.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models, improved UI performance, and enhanced API endpoints.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.x, ensure to migrate your data using the provided migration scripts.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with your Flowise pods listed as '1/1' and 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
